zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Bois於1223年出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾索普 阿諾德時，她的父親Ernald de Bois VI33歲，母親Joan Beauchamp31歲。她於1245年與Sir Thomas de Astley在英國萊希斯特斯爾鮑頓 艾斯利結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1260年在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾努聶亞頓享年37歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Bois于1223年出生在英国萊希斯特斯爾索普 阿諾德时，她的父亲Ernald de Bois VI33岁，母亲Joan Beauchamp31岁。她于1245年与Sir Thomas de Astley在英国萊希斯特斯爾鮑頓 艾斯利结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1260年在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾努聶亞頓享年37岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bois
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

variants.name: Dubois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Pierre, Fernand, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Michel, Alain, Luc.

French, Walloon, and English (of French Huguenot origin): topographic name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ), or a habitational name for someone from any of several places in various parts of France and in Belgium (Wallonia) named Le Bois. This is the most frequent surname in Wallonia. It is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium, the Netherlands, Germany, and Haiti. In both England and North America it is sometimes translated into English as Wood . Compare Deboe , Debois , Deboy , Duboise , and Dubose .

History: This surname is listed (in the form Du Bois) in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. One of the several Huguenot ancestors bearing this name was Chrétien Du Bois from Flanders, whose son, Louis Du Bois (born 1626 in Lille, France, died 1696 in Kingston, NY), was among the Huguenots who founded the town of New Paltz, NY 1675–78.

variants.name: Boisvert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Armand, Andre, Donat, Lucien, Jacques, Gaston, Normand, Pierre, Gilles, Herve, Adrien.
French:
topographic name for someone who lived in a dense forest or perhaps a copse of evergreens, from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + vert, verd ‘green’.

variants.name: Boisclair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Marcel, Aldege, Alphonse, Arsene, Edmour, Emile, Leopaul, Prudent, Rejean, Remi, Yves.

French: topographic name from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + clair ‘clear, light’, or a habitational name from (Le) Bois Clair, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is virtually non-existing in France.

variants.name: Boies
meanings: Altered form of French   Bois  .

variants.name: Baskerville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Boscherville in Eure, France, named with Old Anglo-Norman French boschet ‘copse, thicket’ (a diminutive of Bois ) + ville ‘settlement, town’.

variants.name: Debois
meanings: Altered form of French   Dubois  , a cognate of 1 above.
French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ).

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Bois was born in 1223, in Thorpe Arnold, Leicestershire, England, her father, Ernald de Bois VI, was 33 and her mother, Joan Beauchamp, was 31. She married Sir Thomas de Astley in 1245, in Broughton Astley, Leicestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1260, in Nuneaton, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 37.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Bois est née en 1223, à Thorpe Arnold, Leicestershire, Angleterre, son père, Ernald de Bois VI, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Joan Beauchamp, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas de Astley en 1245, à Broughton Astley, Leicestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1260, à Nuneaton, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 37 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Bois 1223 in Thorpe Arnold, Leicestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Ernald de Bois VI 33 und ihre Mutter Joan Beauchamp 31. Sie hat 1245 in Broughton Astley, Leicestershire, England Sir Thomas de Astley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1260 im Alter von 37 Jahren in Nuneaton, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Bois è nata il nel 1223 nel Thorpe Arnold, Leicestershire, England, sua padre, Ernald de Bois VI, aveva 33 anni e sua madre, Joan Beauchamp, aveva 31 anni. Lei sposato Sir Thomas de Astley nel 1245, nel Broughton Astley, Leicestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1260, nel Nuneaton, Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 37 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Boisが1223年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアソルペ アルノルドで生まれた時，父Ernald de Bois VIは33歳，母Joan Beauchampは31歳であった。1245年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアブロウトン アストリーでSir Thomas de Astleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1260年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアヌネアトンで37歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Bois(이)가 1223년 영국 레이스스터실르 소 아노드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Ernald de Bois VI(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Beauchamp(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1245년 영국 레이스스터실르 브루그h턴 애스틀리에서 Sir Thomas de Astley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1260년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 누니앳에서 37세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Bois nasceu em 1223, em Thorpe Arnold, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Ernald de Bois VI, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Joan Beauchamp, tinha 31 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas de Astley em 1245, em Broughton Astley, Leicestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1260, em Nuneaton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 37 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Bois родилась в 1223 г. в местности таурп арнолд, лисестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Ernald de Bois VI было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Joan Beauchamp, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas de Astley в 1245 г. в местности броугтон астли, лисестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1260 г. в местности нунитон, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 37 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Bois nació en 1223, en Thorpe Arnold, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Ernald de Bois VI, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Joan Beauchamp, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Sir Thomas de Astley en 1245, en Broughton Astley, Leicestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1260, en Nuneaton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 37 años.
